assertions.reason: With valid org-aware sessions, the dashboard rendered distinct role-specific navigation for all six roles. Owner uniquely showed Billing; admin removed Billing; manager removed the admin section; senior_carer exposed My Rota and Audit Log; carer exposed My Persons/Daily Tasks/My Schedule; viewer exposed a read-only Reports path.

Direct navigation to /rbacui-org-a-20260401/settings/billing as a carer did not return 403 or an Access Denied page. The app redirected to /login?callbackUrl=%2Frbacui-org-a-20260401%2Fsettings%2Fbilling instead.
The multi-org user saw an organisation switcher containing both Org A and Org B, with Org A highlighted as the active organisation and per-org roles shown in the dropdown.

Selecting Org B from the switcher did not land on a working Org B dashboard. The UI navigated to /rbacui-org-b-20260401/dashboard, which returns a 404 page, so the required switched dashboard state never appeared through the real flow.

The end-to-end escalation flow is not reliable through the real UI. Before refresh, the promoted user's existing session still rendered the Org A team page as a carer/read-only view. Owner promotion updated the membership row but the UI action threw a 500 because audit_logs expects a missing ip_address column. After attempting a session refresh via the org switcher, /api/auth/session still reported Org A role=carer with stale memberships instead of the promoted admin role.

frictions.description: Credential sign-in for seeded organisation members redirected to /onboarding because the session lacked activeOrgId, so dashboard RBAC assertions could not be exercised from the login form alone.
frictions.resolution: Seeded isolated org-aware session cookies for the assigned test users after creating only namespace-scoped users/orgs/memberships.

frictions.description: The organisation switcher button was not exposed in agent-browser's default interactive snapshot output.
frictions.resolution: Opened it via the DOM selector button[aria-label^='Current organisation'] and then continued the real UI flow.

frictions.description: Changing a member role from the Team page triggers a server-side 500 after the membership update because audit_logs inserts target a missing ip_address column.

notes: Observed browser console/page errors: none on the dashboard/switcher flows; the promotion flow failed at the server action layer and is captured in the shared dev-server log excerpt.
The switcher dropdown correctly displayed both organisations and their roles, but switching routes target missing /[orgSlug]/dashboard pages.
The saved session evidence file shows that the post-refresh session still reported Org A role=carer and stale memberships even after the user had been promoted in the database.
summary: Tested 5 assertions: 2 passed (VAL-AUTH-016, VAL-AUTH-019) and 3 failed (VAL-AUTH-017, VAL-AUTH-020, VAL-CROSS-008).
